findings.evidence: `nl` shows the brittle proof at lines 88-107 and the factored lemma immediately below at lines 113-124; line 123 already proves the same compatibility with a short `rw [Equiv.apply_symm_apply, deckEquivFiberOfSurjective_apply, ← fiberMap_smul, deckEquivFiberOfSurjective_symm_smul]`.
findings.fix: Move `deckEquivFiberOfSurjective_symm_fiberMap` before this lemma, then prove the `/ₛ` statement by rewriting with `deckEquivFiberOfSurjective_symm_eq_sdiv` and applying that lemma with `MulAction.surjective_smul`; keep the low-level calc in only one place.
findings.issue: `fiberMap_sdiv_eq_conjMulEquiv_of_pretransitive` manually rewrites both `/ₛ` terms and proves equality through a hardwired `eq_of_fiber_smul_eq_fiber_smul`/`calc` over `deckEquivFiberOfSurjective`, even though `deckEquivFiberOfSurjective_symm_fiberMap` at lines 113-124 is exactly the reusable inverse-transport fact this argument needs.

summary: One local torsor-division proof still contains the brittle deck-equivalence calc that the file later packages as a reusable transport lemma.
